steps.title: Match subject and tense
steps.content: The subject is the Wheeling National Heritage Area, a singular noun phrase. The sentence describes what that area currently is, and the phrase has long been known also points to a continuing present state.

steps.title: Reject tense and agreement errors
steps.content: A uses past tense, which does not fit the current description. C is plural and disagrees with the singular subject. D uses future perfect for a present fact. The remaining choice is singular present tense. The answer is B.
